/ dashboards

Everything a Texas hotel owner wants to know. In one place.

Revenue rankings. RevPAR by submarket. Twenty years of history. No spreadsheets. No lag. No guesswork.

RevPAR rank · Allen · Apr 2026
$ / room
Ranking RevPAR
Rank every hotel in your market by RevPAR — revenue per available room — with year-over-year movement beside each.
Leaderboard · Allen · Apr 2026
Top
Mid
Bottom
Revenue Table Leaderboard
A ranked leaderboard with at-a-glance flags — green for the market leaders, red for the bottom — so standing reads in one second.
RevPAR ranked low→high · Allen · Apr 2026
color = RevPAR tier
RevPAR Trophy Assets
Your market's RevPAR ladder, shaded red-to-green — instantly see the trophy assets at the top and the laggards at the base.
Allen · monthly revenue · 2026
$ room revenue
Revenue Table
The core grid: every property's room revenue by month and quarter, with the running total column highlighted for fast scanning.
Statewide · monthly · 2014–2026
green = higher
Revenue Heat Map
Twelve years of statewide revenue as a calendar heat map — seasonality runs across, long-run growth runs down, the 2020 shock is unmistakable.
Statewide YoY% · monthly
red − / green +
YoY% Revenue Table
Year-over-year growth as a diverging heat map — red for decline, green for gains. The 2020 collapse and 2021 rebound jump straight out.
Total revenue · Austin · 2026
$ total revenue
Revenue Trend
Total monthly revenue across the market — spot seasonality, peak months, and the slow stretches at a glance.
Allen · quarterly stack · 2023–2026
Taxable
Exempt
YoY%
Revenue Trend (Stacked)
The same revenue, split taxable vs. tax-exempt and stacked quarter by quarter — watch the long-stay (exempt) band thicken over time.
Allen · share of revenue · by quarter
each band = 1 hotel
Revenue Marketshare
Every competitor's slice of total market revenue, normalised to 100% — see who is gaining share and who is giving it up, quarter over quarter.
Allen · revenue by property · 2023–2026
1 line per address
Location Address Revenue Trend
Each property's revenue plotted over time on one axis — the market leader separates from the pack and the gaps stay visible month to month.
Revenue by city · 2021–2026
Allen
Alice
City Revenue Trend
Total room revenue for each city as a filled trend — compare whole markets side by side and catch event spikes the moment they land.
Texas regions · RevPAR & YoY%
RevPAR data bars
City Groups Revenue Table
Roll the whole state up into regional groups — compare RevPAR and growth across the Texas Triangle, Hill Country, oil patch and coast at a glance.
Highest-RevPAR cities · Apr 2026
RevPAR · low supply
City Revenue Untapped Opportunities
Small markets posting outsized RevPAR on just a handful of hotels — the thinly-supplied, high-yield corners worth a closer look.
Statewide RevPAR · 2000–2026
$ / available room
City RevPAR Trend
Twenty-six years of RevPAR for a whole market on one line — the long climb, the oil-boom bump and the 2020 notch all in a single view.
RevPAR percentile · Apr 2026
percentile rank
City Name RevPAR Strength
Each city graded against the whole state — a strength badge plus its exact RevPAR percentile, so 'how strong is this market?' has a number.
RevPAR by property · Allen · 2023–2026
9 hotels · monthly
Location Address RevPAR Trend
Overlay RevPAR lines for your full competitive set. See who's pulling ahead, who's falling behind, and exactly where you stand each month.
# hotels by RevPAR bin · statewide
distribution
RevPAR Bin & Address Distribution
How the entire state's hotels distribute across RevPAR bands — a right-skewed curve that shows where the bulk of properties sit and where the rare high earners are.
RevPAR Map · Texas markets
color = RevPAR tier
RevPAR Map
Every Texas market color-coded by RevPAR. Instantly spot where the strong markets are — and where the hidden opportunities live.
Allen · revenue vs RevPAR · Apr 2026
size = rooms
RevPAR & Revenue Scatterplot
Plot every hotel by total revenue against RevPAR, sized by room count — separate the big-but-thin earners from the small-but-efficient ones.
Allen · RevPAR vs YoY% · Apr 2026
size = rooms
RevPAR & YoY% RevPAR Scatterplot
RevPAR against its own year-over-year change — instantly separate the high-and-rising winners from high-but-fading assets losing momentum.
Median RevPAR by chain scale · statewide
RevPAR data bars
Chain Scale Metrics
The market broken out by chain scale, Luxury down to Economy — see how RevPAR and growth step down the ladder and where each tier really sits.
# locations by franchise · 2010–2026
1 line per brand
Franchise Count Trend
Brand footprints over time — track how many locations each franchise operates statewide and which flags are expanding or pulling back.
Texas geographic coverage
Every reporting city, every zip code, every chain scale across the state.
Property-level precision
Not just city averages — individual hotel addresses, names, and room counts.
Competitive set benchmarking
Compare against your real comp set — not arbitrary market averages.
/ market community

The data tells you what happened.
Operators tell you why.Beta

A members-only feed built into your dashboard — a channel for every Texas market. Ask why a city went soft, compare notes on a strange month, hear it straight from operators on the ground.

Community
#Global #Statewide #Housekeeping
#Abilene #Houston #Lubbock #Midland #SanAngelo #Waco
#Midland Live
Message #Midland
The only room where every voice runs a Texas hotel.
A channel for every market
#Statewide plus 800+ city channels — Abilene to Zapata. Follow the markets you operate in, skip the rest.
Members only — no tourists
Every post comes from behind the paywall. People with real properties and real numbers on the line, not vendors fishing for leads.
Anonymous by default
You post as your initials — no names, no property attached. Talk numbers freely without showing your hand to the comp set.
Included with every membership.
/ how it works

See where you really rank.
In about thirty seconds.

Full access during the trial. Cancel anytime in those 7 days and you're never charged. Every dashboard. Every Texas hotel. Updated monthly.

01
Start your 7-day free trial
Email, password, card details for after the trial. About thirty seconds via Stripe checkout. Cancel anytime in the first 7 days and you're not charged a cent.
02
Pick your view
Browse 20+ dashboards from the left menu. Each report explains itself in the side panel — what it shows, how to read it, how to act on it.
03
Filter, compare, benchmark
Narrow by city, zip, chain scale, or hotel name. Export what you need. Come back every month when the new data drops.
/ pricing

One plan. Everything in.

Built by a Texas hotel owner, for Texas hotel owners. No tiers, no upsells, no surprises.

7 days free · Cancel anytime, no charge
All-access membership
$19.99/mo
After your free trial · Billed monthly
Built for Texas hotel owners
7-day free trial — full access
All 20+ dashboards — every one, no locks
5,500+ Texas hotels, updated monthly
Full history back to 2000
Scatterplots, heat maps, chain scale analytics
Cancel anytime from your account, no charge

Data sourced from Texas State Comptroller Hotel Occupancy Tax public filings. Payments processed by Stripe.

/ who it's for

If you operate a hotel in Texas, this was built for you.

Owners, GMs, and asset managers all ask the same questions every month. We put the answers in one place.

General Managers
Track your competitive set every month. See who in your market is gaining share, losing it, or pulling above-market RevPAR — without waiting on a STAR report.
Hotel Owners
See exactly where your property ranks against comparable hotels in your city, zip, or chain scale. Spot revenue gaps you couldn't see before.
Asset Managers
Pull monthly portfolio reporting from public State filings. Same numbers your GMs report — independently sourced — for every property you manage.
Brokers & Investors
Twenty years of historical revenue and RevPAR data for underwriting. Identify trending submarkets, validate seller numbers, and benchmark acquisitions.

Don't be the last one
to see your rank.

The data is public. Your competitors look at it. Take 30 seconds to start your trial — cancel anytime in those 7 days, no charge.
